title: Price elasticity of demand
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS — PED, YED, XED, revenue and elasticity with GeoGebra supply-demand shift model.
learningObjectives: Calculate and interpret price elasticity of demand (PED).
Explain income elasticity (YED) and cross elasticity (XED).
Relate PED to total revenue changes.
Identify elastic, inelastic, and unit elastic demand.

simpleExplanation.title: How responsive is demand?
simpleExplanation.summary: Elasticity measures percentage response to a percentage change. PED tells firms whether cutting price raises or lowers total revenue.
steps: PED = (% ΔQd)/(% ΔP) — usually negative; report |PED| for magnitude.
Elastic + price fall → revenue rises; inelastic + price fall → revenue falls.
YED: normal good > 0; inferior < 0. XED: substitutes > 0; complements < 0.

sections.content: **Elasticity** is the highest-frequency calculation topic on 9708 AS Paper 2. Combine **formula**, **diagram** (steep vs flat), and **revenue** analysis for full marks.
PED = (ΔQ/Q)/(ΔP/P) | Total revenue TR = P × Q | Elastic: P and TR move in opposite directions

sections.question: Price rises from £4 to £5; quantity falls from 200 to 160. Find PED.
sections.solution:
ΔP = +25%, ΔQ = −20%

PED = (−20%)/(+25%) = **−0.8** (inelastic)

TR before = £800; after = £800 — unchanged direction confirms inelastic range near unit.

sections.content: Use **midpoint formula** if the question specifies arc elasticity. State whether demand is elastic or inelastic **and** explain the revenue implication.

steps.caption: PED = (% ΔQd)/(% ΔP) — elastic if |PED| > 1.
steps.embedHint: Compare steep vs flat demand — steep means inelastic.

steps.caption: Elastic demand: quantity responds strongly to price change.
steps.embedHint: Large quantity change for small price move → elastic.

steps.caption: YED: normal good YED > 0; inferior good YED < 0.
steps.embedHint: Relate income shifts to demand curve movement.

steps.caption: XED > 0 substitutes; XED < 0 complements.
steps.embedHint: Predict cross-price effects from curve shifts.
